title: ZIP-32 test vector via Python oracle — defend against roundtrip-only testing
description:
The wallet.rs test for ZIP-32 master key derivation uses a hardcoded expected value computed by Python's hashlib.blake2b. This is the correct approach: it tests that the Rust implementation produces the same output as an independent implementation of the same spec.

A future contributor might suggest 'simplifying' this to a roundtrip test (derive a key, serialize it, deserialize it, check equality). That test proves nothing about correctness — it only tests that the encode/decode roundtrip works. The current test would catch a bug in the BLAKE2b personalization (wrong constant, wrong output length, wrong key vs. personalization field), which a roundtrip test would never catch.
close_reason: Already implemented and documented correctly in code
